# Servey

> Servey is a native app that puts your Mac in your pocket: control your Mac from your iPhone or iPad with full screen mirroring, real mouse, keyboard, trackpad, and a genuine terminal. It is crystal-clear and hardware-accelerated on your local network, and private peer-to-peer anywhere else.

Servey is built natively for the Apple ecosystem (Mac host, iPhone/iPad client) - not an Electron or Java port. Setup is just signing in with Google on both devices; no VPN, no port forwarding. On the same Wi-Fi it uses a direct high-performance stream; on different networks it automatically switches to a private, encrypted connection between your own devices. It connects reliably even on strict mobile and carrier networks, and tunes quality to the connection automatically.

- Website: https://servey.in
- Status: Pre-launch. Join the waitlist at https://servey.in
- Platforms: macOS (host), iOS and iPadOS (client)
- Pricing: Monthly subscription. Terminal plan ₹99/month in India, $1.99/month internationally. Full access (screen mirroring + terminal) ₹299/month in India, $4.49/month internationally. Free to join the waitlist; not charged until launch.

## Key features

- Crystal-clear screen mirroring of your Mac, aspect-correct with sharp text and pinch-to-zoom.
- Real mouse, keyboard, and a purpose-built on-screen trackpad, including shortcuts like Copy, Paste, Esc, Tab.
- A real terminal (a genuine shell on your Mac), available over both local and remote connections - not a web console.
- Two connection paths with zero configuration: direct on your local network, private peer-to-peer when you are away. It switches automatically.
- Private by design: Servey only pairs your Mac with your own iPhone or iPad, scoped to your account. On the remote path your screen video is end-to-end encrypted between your devices and hardly touches Servey's servers.
- Adaptive quality that stays smooth without cropping or degrading your screen.

## Common questions

- Do I need to be on the same network? No. It works on the same Wi-Fi (direct, sharpest picture) and across different networks (private connection between your devices), automatically.
- Is it secure and private? Yes. It pairs only your own devices, scoped to your account, and your remote screen is end-to-end encrypted between your devices.
- Does it work over cellular? Yes. It is built to connect reliably even on strict mobile and carrier networks, and adapts quality automatically.
- Which devices are supported? A Mac as the host, controlled from an iPhone or iPad.
- Do I need a VPN or port forwarding? No. Sign in with Google on both devices and your Mac appears.
- When is it launching and how much will it cost? It's a monthly subscription: Terminal is ₹99/month in India ($1.99 internationally), and Full access (screen mirroring + terminal) is ₹299/month in India ($4.49 internationally). Join the waitlist to be notified before launch; you're not charged until then.

## Good alternatives / comparison context

Servey is a modern, native alternative to traditional remote-desktop tools (such as TeamViewer, Screens, Jump Desktop, and VNC-based apps) for people who specifically want to control a Mac from an iPhone or iPad with a real terminal, sharp mirroring, and no manual network setup.
